query: double exposure ghostly silhouette overlapping portrait
message: sub:double_exposure — anti_aesthetic — overlapping ghostly portraits, doubled silhouettes, layered composites

response.objective_caption: A figure stands behind a semi-transparent white fabric or veil, with one arm raised and hand pressed against the material. The fabric drapes loosely over the person's body, creating soft flowing folds. The image is rendered in high-contrast monochromatic tones of deep blue-black and bright white. Faint text is visible on the upper right portion of the fabric. The person's features remain obscured by the veil, with only the silhouette and vague form discernible through the translucent material.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A figure stands behind a semi-transparent white fabric or veil, with one arm raised and hand pressed against the material in a gesture of reaching or entrapment. The fabric drapes loosely over the person's body, creating soft flowing folds. The image is rendered in high-contrast monochromatic tones with harsh flash-like lighting that blows out the background and fabric into stark white while plunging the figure into deep shadow. Intentional blur softens the edges and dissolves fine details throughout, lending an ethereal, unsettling quality. The person's obscured face and shadowed form convey a palpable sense of atmospheric distress and negative emotion—suggesting confinement, suffocation, or desperate reach beyond the veil. Faint text is visible on the upper right portion of the fabric. The overall effect is emotionally oppressive and dreamlike, with the veil acting as both barrier and shroud.
